summary_text: Hermes memory sidecar is unlikely to beat incremental improvements to `compact` / `context_reference` / `memory-query` if implementation time is scarce. The ROI case for Hermes is weaker because the repo already has the right primitives: canonical state stays local, `context_reference` already externalizes pointers, and `memory-query` can be improved without adding a second retrieval system. A sidecar only wins if cross-run recall is the dominant bottleneck and you can measure a clear gain fast, like a material reduction in resume-token load or recovery friction. Decision: prioritize tightening the existing mechanisms first. Use Hermes only as a later, narrow read-through layer if the current stack cannot deliver a measurable improvement after a small refactor.
